Title: Polyacylated Delphinidin Biosynthesis Catalysed by PhF3′5′H-PhBGLU12-PhSCPL2 Determines the Blue Pigmentation in Cineraria (Pericallis hybrida)

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme for delphinidin-based anthocyanin formation; rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: flavonoid B-ring hydroxylation substrate leading to delphinidin formation (exact substrate not explicitly stated)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces 3′ and 5′ hydroxyl groups on the flavonoid B-ring to enable formation of delphinidin-based anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms core precursor/backbone for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ray florets and abaxial leaf side/leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: confers pure and stable blue pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed in blue variety ray florets; confirmed high in PeB ray florets and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in blue cineraria ray florets and leaves; low/absent in pink cultivar tissues examined
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S or transient overexpression vector promoter not explicitly stated; endogenous promoter used in LUC reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber, 22–24°C, 60% humidity; transient silencing or overexpression assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS/transient silencing in PeB leaves; co-overexpression with PhBGLU12 in PeP leaves; RT-qPCR; UPLC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; Y1H/EMSA/dual-luciferase for upstream regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes delphinidin backbone formation and enables accumulation of polyacylated delphinidin derivatives, leading to blue pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhF3′5′H is specifically expressed in blue cineraria tissues accumulating delphinidin-based pigments. 2. Silencing PhF3′5′H in PeB reduced anthocyanin content and caused leaf fading. 3. Co-expression of PhF3′5′H with PhBGLU12 in pink cineraria enabled accumulation of multiple polyacylated delphinidin derivatives and blue spots.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful core branch gene for engineering delphinidin production; should be stacked with modification genes such as PhBGLU12 and likely endogenous/compatible acylation capacity to achieve stable blue coloration
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude PhF3′5′H alone is insufficient for complex polyacylated delphinidin accumulation; requires downstream modification steps
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/full-length transcriptome mentioned; accession not provided

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BGLU family; clusters with DgAA7GT and DcAA5GT
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key modification enzyme for second glycosylation at 7-position; rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 7-position anthocyanin intermediate lacking the second glycosylation step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 7-position second glycosylated anthocyanin intermediate enabling further polyacylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the second glycosylation step at the 7-position of anthocyanins, supporting formation of polyacylated delphinidin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate decorated precursor required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: polyacylated delphinidin derivatives (e.g., cinerarin-related components B2/B3/B4)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stabilizes anthocyanins and shifts color toward blue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in blue cultivar tissues; induced in transient OE lines in pink leaves
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PhMYB6; functional pathway cooperation with PhSCPL2
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi and transient OE vector promoters not explicitly stated; endogenous promoter tested in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth conditions; transient silencing, stable RNAi, and transient co-overexpression contexts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient silencing in PeB leaves; stable RNAi in PeB; transient co-overexpression with PhF3′5′H in PeP; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; UPLC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; Y1H/EMSA/dual-luciferase for transcriptional regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes second 7-position glycosylation, increasing polyacylated delphinidin derivatives and blue coloration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhBGLU12 is highly expressed in blue cineraria and localizes to the tonoplast. 2. Silencing PhBGLU12 caused fading/pale purple phenotypes and reduced B2/B3/B4 while increasing B1, supporting a role in the second 7-position glycosylation step. 3. Co-expression with PhF3′5′H in pink leaves enabled accumulation of multiple polyacylated delphinidin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important modifier gene to pair with F3′5′H and acylation capacity for creation of stable blue ornamental varieties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer function from phylogeny and metabolite shifts rather than direct in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/full-length transcriptome mentioned; accession not provided

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 7- and 3′-position caffeoylation module of delphinidin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key acylation enzyme for 7- and 3′-position caffeoylation; rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: glucosylated anthocyanin intermediates at 7 and 3′ positions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates caffeoylation modifications at the 7- and 3′-positions of anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: intensifies blue hue and enhances pigment stability
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in PeB and also high in PeP according to authors
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi vector promoter not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth conditions; stable RNAi context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable RNAi in PeB; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; UPLC-MS/MS structural elucidation of new metabolite B0
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes caffeoylation at 7 and 3′ positions, enabling blue shift and accumulation of cinerarin-like polyacylated pigments
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhSCPL2 localizes to the tonoplast and is expressed in anthocyanin-accumulating cultivars. 2. RNAi silencing turned blue ray florets lavender and leaves greyish-purple. 3. Silencing reduced original anthocyanin components and generated B0 lacking 7- and 3′-acylation, indicating PhSCPL2 catalyzes caffeoylation at both positions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical acylation gene for engineering stable blue flowers; should be combined with delphinidin-forming and glycosylation genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains MYB/W-box/G-box/ABRE motifs, but no tested MYB bound it in Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome/full-length transcriptome mentioned; accession not provided

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis, specifically polyacylated delphinidin derivative formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates PhF3′5′H and PhBGLU12 promoters; activates PhWRKY44 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core MYB transcription factor selectively controlling polyacylated anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No convincing protein interaction with PhWRKY44 in plant cells; Y2H unidirectional interaction only
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: ray florets and leaves of blue cineraria
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with anthocyanin-accumulating blue tissues; reduced when PhWRKY44 is silenced
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoters of PhF3′5′H, PhBGLU12, and PhWRKY44 tested in LUC/Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth conditions; transcriptional assays in yeast and tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; expression analysis upon PhWRKY44 silencing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates key structural genes to promote accumulation of polyacylated delphinidin derivatives and blue pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhMYB6 directly binds the promoters of PhF3′5′H and PhBGLU12. 2. It activates both promoters in dual-luciferase assays, promoting polyacylated delphinidin biosynthesis. 3. PhMYB6 also activates PhWRKY44 and forms a positive feedback loop with PhWRKY44.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulatory lever for blue-flower engineering because it coordinately activates branch and modification genes; likely best deployed with intact downstream pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PhWRKY44 (Y2H only, not supported by BiLC in plant cells)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop with PhWRKY44 via mutual transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PhMYB3 binds only PhF3′5′H promoter and is not considered the core regulator for polyacylated delphinidin formation in this study

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group I WRKY with two WRKY domains and two C2H2 zinc finger domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis through activation of upstream MYB regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates PhMYB6 promoter, thereby increasing downstream structural gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Activated by PhMYB6; developmental stage-associated expression correlated with anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important upstream activator that amplifies anthocyanin accumulation through a positive feedback loop with PhMYB6
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No confirmed protein complex with PhMYB6 in plant cells; BiLC negative
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes saturated blue coloration by enhancing anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High at stage S1 in PeP, PeC, and PeB; low in white cultivar
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in pigmented cultivars; silencing reduces anthocyanin-associated gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous PhMYB6 promoter tested in Y1H/EMSA/LUC assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth conditions; transient silencing in leaves; yeast/tobacco transcriptional assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome screening; RT-qPCR; transient silencing in PeB leaves; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and blue pigmentation by activating PhMYB6
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhWRKY44 expression correlates with anthocyanin accumulation and is high in pigmented cultivars. 2. Silencing PhWRKY44 causes fading and reduces PhMYB6 and structural gene expression. 3. PhWRKY44 directly binds and activates the PhMYB6 promoter, while PhMYB6 reciprocally activates PhWRKY44.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream regulator for amplifying blue anthocyanin production; may be combined with PhMYB6-centered engineering for stronger pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback loop with PhMYB6 via mutual transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from full-length transcriptome by expression correlation and phylogeny with AtWRKY44/TTG2-like proteins
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome mentioned; accession not provided
